title: The Future of Publishing Is Agent-Auditable Research
thesis: The future of serious publishing is human-authored research that agents can audit: readable essays backed by claim graphs, evidence ledgers, provenance, revision history, and disclosed agent involvement.

claims.claim: Polished prose is becoming cheap; inspectable reasoning is becoming scarce.
counterevidence: High-quality human judgment and taste remain hard to automate; the claim is about the marginal cost of competent prose, not the end of authorship.

claims.claim: A future-ready publishing artifact should pair a human essay with a claim graph, evidence ledger, provenance, revision history, and agent contribution record.
counterevidence: The exact schema is not standardized and may vary by community.

claims.claim: Research is the best first wedge because the audience already values citations, methods, uncertainty, and credibility.
counterevidence: Mainstream publishing may adopt agent workflows faster if distribution incentives are stronger.

claims.claim: Existing publishing, AI research, and protocol tools solve important pieces but not the combined readable-plus-auditable source object.
counterevidence: Some tools may evolve into this combined artifact quickly.

claims.claim: AI assistance should be disclosed while the human remains accountable for thesis, source selection, wording, and conclusions.
counterevidence: Disclosure norms differ by venue and are still evolving.

claims.claim: Attention-aware reading and machine-readable structure are compatible when the page uses progressive disclosure.
counterevidence: Badly implemented progressive disclosure can hide important context.

sources.title: Agentic Publications: An LLM-Driven Framework for Interactive Scientific Publishing
Responsible Agentic AI Requires Explicit Provenance
From Abilities to AI Agents: Introducing the WordPress MCP Adapter
A Brief History & Ethos of the Digital Garden
4 Principles for Reducing Cognitive Load in Forms

agentInstructions: Use claims as the retrieval unit.
Prefer source IDs over inline citation guessing.
Treat maturity=seed as an explicit uncertainty marker.
Do not infer that Kimi WebBridge contributed; it was unavailable during implementation.

humanReview.notes: Initial seed article shaped through cross-agent review. Human author retains final judgment before public launch.
